sourcesContent:
# composition matrix rows for implicit objects:
# x = a: 1 → x = {a: 1} — single-line and indented-block objects,
# object-in-call (`f a: 1`), nesting with implicit calls in property
# values, nested key objects, objects in arrays/parens/interpolations,
# keyword and string/number keys, member access on parenthesized
# objects, and postfix guards ending the object before the IF.
mk = (o) -> o
pick = (o) -> o.a

# assignment forms: single pair, multiple pairs, spaced colon
p1 = a: 1
p2 = a: 1, b: 2
p3 = a : 3

# object-in-call: whole-argument object, trailing pairs after a value
c1 = mk a: 1, b: 2
c2 = mk 1, a: 2
c3 = mk a: 1, 2

# nested keys and explicit-brace interleaving
n1 = a: b: 1
n2 = {a: b: 2}

# implicit calls inside property values (the F2/F3 interleaving)
v1 = a: mk 1
v2 = a: 1, b: pick a: 9, c: 3

# indented object bodies; indented pair values; blank lines inside
m1 =
  a: 1
  b: 2
m2 = a:
  4
m3 =
  a:
    b: 5
  c: 6

m4 =
  a: 1

  b: 2

# collections and receivers
l1 = [a: 1, b: 2]
l2 = [{a: 1}, b: 2]
g1 = mk (a: 1), 2
r1 = (a: 7).a
o1 = mk.call null, a: 8

# keyword, string, and number keys
k1 = when: 1, if: 2, for: 3
k2 = "s": 4
k3 = 1: "one"

# ternary colons are not object keys; object values may hold ternaries
t1 = p1.a ? 1 : 2
t2 = k: p1.a ? 3 : 4

# logical operators bind the property value, never end the object
lg = a: 1 && 2

# postfix guard ends the object (and its call) before the IF
h = 0
h = mk a: 5 if p2.b > 0

# implicit object inside an interpolation
msg = "obj: #{(a: 1).a}"
